message.content:
\begin{center}
\Large \textbf{Linear Regression: One-Page Summary}
\end{center}

\section*{Model Setup}

Given data
\[
\{(x_i, y_i)\}_{i=1}^n,\quad x_i \in \mathbb{R}^p,\; y_i \in \mathbb{R},
\]
the linear regression model assumes
\[
y_i = \beta_0 + x_i^\top \beta + \varepsilon_i,\quad \varepsilon_i \sim \text{i.i.d. } (0,\sigma^2).
\]
Using augmented features \(\tilde{x}_i = (1, x_i^\top)^\top \in \mathbb{R}^{p+1}\) and \(\tilde{\beta} = (\beta_0, \beta^\top)^\top\),
\[
y_i = \tilde{x}_i^\top \tilde{\beta} + \varepsilon_i.
\]

Stacking observations:
\[
y = X\beta + \varepsilon,
\]
where \(X \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times p}\), \(y \in \mathbb{R}^n\), \(\beta \in \mathbb{R}^p\).

\section*{Ordinary Least Squares (OLS)}

OLS estimates \(\beta\) by minimizing the sum of squared residuals:
\[
\hat{\beta} = \arg\min_{\beta} \; S(\beta), \quad S(\beta) = \sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - x_i^\top \beta)^2
= \|y - X\beta\|_2^2.
\]

Taking derivatives and setting to zero:
\[
\frac{\partial S}{\partial \beta}
= -2X^\top(y - X\beta) = 0
\quad \Rightarrow \quad
X^\top X \hat{\beta} = X^\top y.
\]

If \(X^\top X\) is invertible,
\[
\hat{\beta} = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y.
\]

\section*{Geometric Interpretation}

The fitted values are
\[
\hat{y} = X\hat{\beta} = P_X y, \quad
P_X = X(X^\top X)^{-1}X^\top.
\]
\(P_X\) is the orthogonal projection matrix onto the column space of \(X\). The residuals
\[
\hat{\varepsilon} = y - \hat{y}
\]
are orthogonal to all columns of \(X\):
\[
X^\top \hat{\varepsilon} = 0.
\]

\section*{Statistical Properties (Classical Assumptions)}

Assume
\[
y = X\beta + \varepsilon,\quad \mathbb{E}[\varepsilon]=0,\quad \operatorname{Var}(\varepsilon)=\sigma^2 I_n.
\]
Then
\[
\hat{\beta} = (X^\top X)^{-1}X^\top y
= \beta + (X^\top X)^{-1}X^\top \varepsilon.
\]

Unbiasedness:
\[
\mathbb{E}[\hat{\beta}] = \beta.
\]

Variance:
\[
\operatorname{Var}(\hat{\beta})
= (X^\top X)^{-1}X^\top \sigma^2 I_n X (X^\top X)^{-1}
= \sigma^2 (X^\top X)^{-1}.
\]

Error variance estimator:
\[
\hat{\sigma}^2 = \frac{1}{n-p}\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - \hat{y}_i)^2
= \frac{\|\hat{\varepsilon}\|_2^2}{n-p}.
\]

\section*{Inference}

For component \(j\),
\[
\hat{\beta}_j \sim \mathcal{N}\left(\beta_j,\, \sigma^2 [(X^\top X)^{-1}]_{jj}\right)
\quad \text{(under normal errors)}.
\]
Estimate standard error:
\[
\operatorname{SE}(\hat{\beta}_j) = \sqrt{\hat{\sigma}^2 [(X^\top X)^{-1}]_{jj}}.
\]
A \(100(1-\alpha)\%\) confidence interval:
\[
\hat{\beta}_j \pm t_{n-p,\,1-\alpha/2
